title: The Problem Solvers | Max Junestrand at Legora
summary: Legora is an AI-native operating system that enables legal teams to autonomously plan, execute, review, and complete complex legal work end-to-end. The platform is built to leverage model improvements to transform legal work processes, with founders working closely with Anthropic.
writeup: Legora's founder describes how the company is building an AI-native legal workspace that integrates document review, drafting, and research into a single platform, with Claude (especially Opus) as the core model. The central argument is that legal work is uniquely dependent on nuanced language and reasoning, and that no prior technology matched those demands the way large language models now do. The through-line is a strategy of riding each successive Anthropic model release rather than building around a fixed capability ceiling.

key_points: Claude Opus is cited specifically for fine-grained drafting and review, where matching the style of an existing document or a particular lawyer's voice is the critical capability no earlier tool could provide.

Legora's product strategy treats each new model release as a rising tide: the team invests in building infrastructure that automatically improves as Anthropic ships new models, rather than optimizing around a static baseline.
The company frames its competitive differentiation as velocity, the claim being that firms betting on Legora get a partner committed to the highest rate of improvement in the legal AI market.
The founder positions the shift to LLMs as a structural break in legal technology history, arguing that language and reasoning were the core bottleneck and that bottleneck is now removed.
